cf_parsed.subtask_body:
I have developed a significantly optimized schedule by reordering Factory A to minimize the completion times of key jobs, particularly Job 2 and Job 4, and then meticulously scheduling Factory B to minimize the idle time of the bottleneck machine MB6.
In the new Factory A schedule, Job 2 finishes at T=29 and Job 4 finishes at T=37, which allows the Factory B pipeline to start much earlier.
The bottleneck machine in Factory B, MB6, is now more efficiently utilized, handling jobs in the sequence J2, J4, J5, J3, J1.
The resulting makespan is 100, which is an improvement over the previous 107 and much better than the baseline 155.
All job-shop constraints, machine exclusivity, and inter-factory (A-to-B) dependencies are strictly respected.
The final finish times for Factory B are: J1 at T=100, J2 at T=57, J3 at T=97, J4 at T=83, and J5 at T=89.
The critical path for J1 in Factory B ends at T=100 on MB2.
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state:
Re-optimize Factory A to shift all job completion times as early as possible.
Use the updated completion times to refine the Factory B schedule.
Focus on the bottleneck machine MB6 in Factory B, ensuring it starts as early as possible with minimal gaps.
Verify all job-order and machine-exclusivity constraints are met.
Target a makespan of 100 or less.

parsed.subtask_body:
I have constructed a new schedule for both factories, focusing on minimizing the idle time of the bottleneck machines MA5 (Factory A) and MB6 (Factory B).
In Factory A, I compacted operations to finish all components by T=54, with J1-J5 finishing at 29, 37, 47, 54, and 47 respectively.
In Factory B, I scheduled MB6 starting from T=33, and then placed other operations using a greedy Earliest Start Time (EST) approach that respects both job sequences and machine exclusivity.
The resulting makespan is 107, which is a significant improvement over the baseline 155.
Key sequences for MB6 are J1[33-35], J2[37-44], J3[70-78], J5[78-86], and J4[86-95].
Key sequences for MB4 (another high-load machine) are J1[29-33], J5[47-56], J4[56-65], J2[65-68], and J3[68-70].
All job-shop constraints and inter-factory dependencies (A-to-B) are satisfied.
parsed.updated_plan_state:
Identify bottleneck machines (MA5 in Factory A, MB6 in Factory B).
Shift Factory A operations earlier by overlapping jobs; aim to finish all Factory A jobs by T=50.
Set release times for Factory B jobs equal to their Factory A completion times.
Schedule Factory B jobs by prioritizing MB6 and then other machines using a greedy EST approach.
Maintain a Gantt-style logical grid to prevent machine or job conflicts.
Refine the sequence in both factories to minimize the idle time on MB6 and total makespan.
Target makespan is < 95.

In Factory B, I scheduled MB6 starting from T=33, and then placed other operations using a greedy Earliest Start Time (EST) approach that respects both job sequences and machine exclusivity.
The resulting makespan is 107, which is a significant improvement over the baseline 155.
